prompt.content:
Use file “Cards_Patient 14” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

The patient calls the cardiology office on 1/9/2026, stating that he is still experiencing palpitations. He does not wish to undergo any procedures or change his oral medication regimen. What clinical trial should this patient be considered for? Please explain your reasoning. Answers should be in line with the most recent clinical guidelines as of January 9, 2026.

messages.content: States that the patient should be considered for the ReVeRA-301 clinical trial.

golden_responses.golden_text_response: The patient presented to the cardiology office with symptomatic palpitations consistent with episodic atrial fibrillation as per their Holter monitor. The patient tried beta-blockers (Metoprolol succinate 50 mg BID) without relief in symptoms. The patient is now seeking a way to control their symptomatic AF without procedures or changes to their oral medication regimen. The clinical trial that the patient should be considered for is ReVeRA-301, as it is testing the intranasal Etripamil for possible symptomatic relief in AF patients.
